title: 'Star Wars' Goes Marvel, 'Judy' Goes for Oscar, and J.Lo Goes Running to the Super Bowl
format: A grab-bag of movie news, Kevin Feige's move to Star Wars, a 'Judy' review that turns into a full biopic taxonomy, and an interview with Daniel Scheinert on 'The Death of Dick Long.'

films.title: Clue
films.note: The Tim Curry butler standard Sean holds the Bateman/Reynolds reboot to.

segments.heading: Trailer Talk: Uncut Gems, The Irishman, and Spoiling Yourself

films.title: Uncut Gems
films.note: Sean's watched the trailer seven times; Amanda's avoiding it on purpose.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: Premieres today at the New York Film Festival; its trailer gives away plot, unlike Uncut Gems'.

films.title: Pain and Glory
films.note: Amanda went in cold on Almodóvar's film and was glad she did.

films.title: U-Turn
films.note: Sean revisited the trailer chasing a 15-year-old memory of it.

films.title: The Social Network
films.note: Runner-up in the Ringer's old trailer bracket.

films.title: Inception
films.note: Won the trailer bracket.

films.title: Star Wars: The Force Awakens
films.note: Trailer-bracket final four.

films.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
films.note: Trailer-bracket final four; Sean argues Scorsese basically invented the form.

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: Its teaser had Sean 'jacked and pumped,' per Bobby's report.

segments.heading: Kevin Feige Goes to a Galaxy Far, Far Away

films.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
films.note: Out in two and a half months; closes the Skywalker saga.

films.title: Star Wars: The Last Jedi
films.note: Made $1.3 billion: successful, but $700 million short of The Force Awakens.

films.title: Solo: A Star Wars Story
films.note: Made $350 million worldwide on a $250-300 million budget; the fraught production Sean blames partly on Alden Ehrenreich.

films.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story
films.note: Did well but had its own complicated production.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: The comparison point for how to retire a decade of characters.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2
films.note: Amanda: taken for granted.

films.title: How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World
films.note: Sean calls it a sleeper Best Animated Feature contender for Dean DeBlois, who's since moved to The Micronauts.

films.title: The Micronauts
films.note: Dean DeBlois's next project, adapting the 1980s toy line; in pre-production, no release yet.

films.title: Judy
films.note: Rupert Goold's Renée Zellweger-as-Judy-Garland biopic. Both hosts agree: formulaic, oversells the childhood-flashback theme-explaining, but the closing 'Somewhere Over the Rainbow' still got Sean crying before it started.

segments.heading: The Anatomy of a Biopic
segments.note: Judy Garland's back catalog, Renée Zellweger's career, and a full taxonomy of the genre

films.title: The Wizard of Oz
films.note: Both hosts' first Judy Garland memory.

films.title: A Star Is Born
films.note: Home to 'It's a New World,' Sean's favorite Garland vocal performance, a song he says only seven or eight people on earth can sing.

films.title: Meet Me in St. Louis
films.note: Where 'Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas' debuted as a Garland song, not a Sinatra standard.

films.title: Chicago
films.note: Zellweger's other Best Actress nomination, proof she can actually sing.

films.title: A Price Above Rubies
films.note: Zellweger's post-Jerry Maguire indie detour.

films.title: Nurse Betty
films.note: A Neil LaBute film Amanda calls underrated and half-forgotten.

films.title: Cold Mountain
films.note: Zellweger already has an Oscar from this one, which complicates the inevitability of a second.

films.title: Bridget Jones's Diary
films.note: Amanda's caught Zellweger slipping into the British cadence mid-Judy Garland impression.

films.title: Bohemian Rhapsody
films.note: Rami Malek's win is the counterexample to the Academy usually rewarding disappearing-into-a-role over impersonation.

films.title: Ray
films.note: Cradle-to-grave category.

films.title: Malcolm X
films.note: Cradle-to-grave category.

films.title: Ali
films.note: Cradle-to-grave category.

films.title: Evita
films.note: Cradle-to-grave category.

films.title: Milk
films.note: The tragedy category's most famous example.

films.title: I'm Not There
films.note: Impressionistic category: the Dylan movie.

films.title: Vice
films.note: Impressionistic category.

films.title: Jackie
films.note: Impressionistic category.

films.title: Marie Antoinette
films.note: Impressionistic category.

films.title: Walk the Line
films.note: The hagiography category; Sean wishes it were nastier given how dark Johnny Cash's life actually got.

films.title: Joker
films.note: Scuttlebutt has Joaquin Phoenix the Best Actor frontrunner.

films.title: Julie & Julia
films.note: The 'famous person through a normal person's eyes' category.

films.title: The Queen
films.note: Two-hander category: equal weight to Elizabeth and Blair.

films.title: Frost/Nixon
films.note: Another Peter Morgan two-hander.

films.title: The Special Relationship
films.note: Peter Morgan's Blair-and-Clinton two-hander.

films.title: Catch Me If You Can
films.note: Unreliable-narrator category.

films.title: A Beautiful Mind
films.note: Unreliable-narrator category.

films.title: Amadeus
films.note: Unreliable-narrator category.

films.title: Steve Jobs
films.note: The 'snapshot' category: one week standing in for a whole life, Sean's favorite style.

films.title: Lincoln
films.note: Manohla Dargis comparison point for narrowing a life to representative moments.

films.title: Rocketman
films.note: 2019's biopic crop.

films.title: Extremely Wicked, Shockingly Evil and Vile
films.note: 2019's Ted Bundy biopic.

films.title: Fighting with My Family
films.note: Amanda's not sure a WWE biopic even counts as one.

films.title: Tolkien
films.note: Amanda calls it one of the worst movies she's seen this year.

films.title: Dolemite Is My Name
films.note: Both hosts just saw it together.

films.title: Ford v Ferrari
films.note: Still to come this year.

films.title: A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood
films.note: Another one they've just seen.

films.title: Sylvia
films.note: Amanda's go-to for when the format goes wrong: Gwyneth Paltrow as Sylvia Plath opposite Daniel Craig's Ted Hughes.

films.title: The Death of Dick Long
films.note: Scheinert's solo feature (his writing partner Dan Kwan sat this one out) from a Billy Chew script, shot in Alabama on a shoestring.

films.title: Swiss Army Man
films.note: His previous film with Daniel Kwan.

films.title: Wet Hot American Summer
films.note: Turned him off in high school, then became a favorite once he caught up to its jokes.

films.title: Fight Club
films.note: He couldn't finish it the first time either.

films.title: Human Centipede
films.note: The kind of provocation-for-its-own-sake he says he doesn't aspire to.

films.title: Lolita
films.note: The kind of 'this is incredible but it shouldn't be' filmmaking he does aspire to.

films.title: Election
films.note: Referenced via a documentary about its production and a school that regretted letting it film there.

films.title: The Sixth Sense
films.note: Sean jokingly "spoils" it after Scheinert's Dick Long spoiler tangent.

films.title: Die Hard
films.note: The joke alternative spoiler.

films.title: John Wick
films.note: His pitch for an unmade sequel where the dog avenges him.

films.title: Midsommar
films.note: His answer to 'last great thing you've seen': saw it twice, loved a couple fighting behind him the second time.

films.title: Sweet Home Alabama
films.note: His example of Hollywood's go-to bad Alabama, shot in Georgia with everyone talking like Gone with the Wind.

films.title: Easy Rider
films.note: The other bad-Alabama touchstone: a trucker shoots people and the credits roll.

Unmade/hypothetical: Everything Everywhere All at Once (in development, joke-titled 'Hot Dog Hands')
How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days 2049 (joke pitch)

Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker

Rogue One: A Star Wars Story.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story

How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden Wor.title: How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World

Extremely Wicked, Shockingly Evil and Vi.title: Extremely Wicked, Shockingly Evil and Vile

A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood.title: A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood
